task: Implemented Signal 3 direction inference by walking handover commit history to detect last coding session, enabling autonomous session routing without timestamp-based false positives.
discussion_summary: Identified critical flaw in timestamp-based handover staleness detection: stop hook commits the handover itself, making any timestamp comparison useless. Pivoted to semantic approach: Signal 3 now diffs consecutive handover commits in git history, looking for non-askr_state file changes to identify the last actual coding session. This breaks the false-positive loop where conversational sessions would incorrectly trigger autonomous routing. User confirmed the approach during implementation.

accomplishments.what: Implemented _infer_direction() in lifecycle.py with Signal 3 logic that walks git history to find last coding commit pair
Tested Signal 3 with real commit history; verified it correctly identifies coding vs. talk-only session pairs
Committed lifecycle.py changes to main branch

in_progress.what: Session log accumulating tool runs and edits; needs final commit before next session

next_actions.action: Commit askr_state/implementation_state.md with final session log
next_actions.why: Uncommitted state file must be clean before next session handover; stop hook will regenerate it

next_actions.action: Test direction_confirm flow end-to-end: trigger Signal 3 in a fresh session, verify input box appears, confirm direction selection opens autonomous session
next_actions.why: Last git log entry indicates this is the next immediate test; validates the full routing pipeline

next_actions.action: If direction_confirm works, stress-test with rapid session cycles (talk → code → talk → code) to ensure Signal 3 correctly disambiguates each transition
next_actions.why: Real-world usage will have mixed session types; need confidence the signal is robust

next_actions.action: Document Signal 3 logic in ARCHITECTURE.md or lifecycle docstring with examples of commit pairs it detects
next_actions.why: Future maintainers need to understand why this approach was chosen over timestamp-based detection

decisions.decision: Signal 3 uses git diff between consecutive handover commits, not file timestamps or session duration
decisions.reason: Timestamp comparison fails because stop hook commits the handover itself; semantic diff on askr_state/ vs. code files is reliable and self-correcting

decisions.decision: Signal 3 only fires if last coding commit pair exists and is recent enough (within momentum window)
decisions.reason: Prevents stale handovers from triggering autonomous routing; conversational sessions leave no code changes, so Signal 3 naturally stays silent

failed_approaches.approach: Timestamp-based staleness check: compare handover creation time to latest commit time
failed_approaches.reason: Stop hook commits the handover immediately after creation, so any commit after handover timestamp is guaranteed — check always passes, defeating the purpose

relational_files.why: Stop hook calls _infer_direction() to determine session type before creating handover
Extension's direction_confirm handler receives direction signal from lifecycle inference
Signal 3 walks git history of handover commits to infer direction; output feeds into next session routing
